channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: PREVIEW: Fruita vs. Central in the softball State Tournament
item.description: “It’s going to be awesome," said the always energetic Fruita head coach Adam Diaz, gleaming with excitement.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: NC State's Grayson McCall gives up football, says he can't come back from latest head injury
item.description: RALEIGH, N.C. (AP) — North Carolina State quarterback Grayson McCall announced his retirement from football on Wednesday night, less than three weeks after a vicious hit knocked him out of a game for the second time this season.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Birthday boy Nick Bosa battling elbow injury ahead of 49ers-Cowboys game
item.description: The 49ers' Nick Bosa expects the Dallas Cowboys to attempt a more run-oriented approach when they visit Levi's Stadium on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Cam Inman, The Mercury News

item.title: Tua Tagovailoa practices for the first time since suffering a concussion in Week 2
item.description: MIAMI GARDENS, Fla. (AP) — Tyreek Hill couldn't hide his excitement catching the football from Tua Tagovailoa on Wednesday for the first time in over a month. “I just had a big cheese on my face,” Hill said. “It just felt so good.
item.dc:creator: ALANIS THAMES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How Fernando Valenzuela changed Los Angeles forever
item.description: The historic impact Fernando Valenzuela made on the city of Los Angeles traces its roots to the 1950s, according to renounced sports commentator Roy Firestone.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KTLA 5

item.title: Fernando Valenzuela, Mexican-born pitcher whose feats for Dodgers fueled 'Fernandomania,' dies at 63
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Fernando Valenzuela, the Mexican-born phenom for the Los Angeles Dodgers who inspired “Fernandomania” while winning the NL Cy Young Award and Rookie of the Year in 1981, has died. He was 63.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Dodgers vs. Yankees: A look back at their World Series history
item.description: The teams will square off in the Fall Classic for the 12th time in a star-studded matchup that begins Friday, but it’s their first October showdown since 1981. The Yankees hold an 8-3 advantage.
item.dc:creator: Bill Plunkett, The Mercury News

item.title: Dodgers phenom Fernando Valenzuela dies at 63
item.description: Fernando Valenzuela, the Mexican-born phenom for the Los Angeles Dodgers who inspired “Fernandomania” while winning the NL Cy Young Award and Rookie of the Year in 1981, has died.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Dodgers phenom Fernando Valenzuela dies at 63, just days before former team faces Yankees in World Series
item.description: Fernando Valenzuela, the Mexican-born phenom for the Los Angeles Dodgers who inspired “Fernandomania” while winning the NL Cy Young Award and Rookie of the Year in 1981, has died.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: LeBron and Bronny James make history as the NBA's first father-son duo to play together
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — LeBron James gave his 20-year-old son a pep talk before they rose from the Lakers bench. Amid rising cheers, they walked together to the scorer's table — and then they stepped straight into basketball history.
item.dc:creator: GREG BEACHAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tatum, Celtics celebrate Banner 18 by making record-tying 29 3-pointers and crushing Knicks 132-109
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — Jayson Tatum continued Boston’s Banner 18 celebration by scoring 37 points and making eight of the Celtics' NBA record-tying 29 3-pointers on Tuesday as Boston ran away with a 132-109 victory over the New York Knicks on opening night.
item.dc:creator: JIMMY GOLEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Stanley Cup champion Panthers and coach Paul Maurice agree on contract extension
item.description: SUNRISE, Fla. (AP) — The contract extension discussions between Paul Maurice and the Florida Panthers weren't exactly of the back-and-forth variety. “There was really no negotiation," Maurice said. "They made me a really nice offer and we shook hands and I said, ‘Thank you.’ And that was it.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: William Nylander scores twice, powers Maple Leafs over Lightning 5-2
item.description: William Nylander had two goals and an assist as the Toronto Maple Leafs defeated the Tampa Bay Lightning 5-2 on Monday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: WNBA players union decides to opt out of current collective bargaining agreement
item.description: The current CBA will still cover the 2025 season so the two sides have a year to negotiate a new agreement.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Russell Wilson throws for 2 TDs, runs for another TD in Pittsburgh debut as Steelers rout Jets 37-15
item.description: PITTSBURGH (AP) — Russell Wilson sat at the podium, uniform still on, in no rush to move on from a moment he believed would always come. Even as the weeks passed and his aching calf struggled to get better. Even as Justin Fields deftly guided the Pittsburgh Steelers in his place.
item.dc:creator: WILL GRAVES, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Andy Ludwig steps down as Utes offensive coordinator
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Following another dismal offensive performance against TCU, Andy Ludwig has stepped down as Utah's offensive coordinator and quarterbacks coach. Utah head coach Kyle Whittingham released a statement Sunday night.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Patrick Mahomes and Chiefs stay undefeated, beat 49ers 28-18 in Super Bowl rematch
item.description: SANTA CLARA, Calif. (AP) — The formula might look a bit different with the defense and running game carrying the Kansas City Chiefs. But the results are awfully familiar as the two-time defending champions keep knocking off teams — especially the San Francisco 49ers.
item.dc:creator: JOSH DUBOW, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Jayden Daniels to get further tests Monday after leaving the Commanders' game with a rib injury
item.description: Jayden Daniels will undergo further tests Monday after leaving the Washington Commanders’ game against the Carolina Panthers with a rib injury, coach Dan Quinn said, repeatedly adding he had no further update on the rookie quarterback's status.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Santa Monica Ferris wheel to light up in support of Dodgers World Series push
item.description: The Los Angeles Dodgers are knocking at the door of the World Series, and one of Los Angeles' most iconic landmarks will be decked out in blue to reflect the city's support.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: Juan Soto's 3-run homer in 10th sends Yankees past Guardians 5-2 and into World Series for 41st time
item.description: CLEVELAND (AP) — Juan Soto was supposed to be the difference-maker, the one who pushed the New York Yankees back to the top. They're one step away.
item.dc:creator: TOM WITHERS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: CMU def. New Mexico Highlands 21-10 in homecoming game
item.description: GRAND JUNCTION, Colo. (KREX)—In the second quarter, with the game tied at zero, the Cowboys sought to break the deadlock. Joey Cave handed the ball off to Mike Jones, who found space after strong blocking from his teammates.
item.dc:creator: Titus Cleveland, KREX 5

item.title: BYU wins last second thriller over Oklahoma State, 38-35
item.description: PROVO, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - The dream season for BYU continues. Jake Retzlaff threw a 35-yard touchdown pass to Darius Lassiter with 10 seconds left and No. 13 BYU remained undefeated, outlasting Oklahoma State 38-35 on Friday night.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4
